channel.title: Joining on smpaget blog
channel.description: Recent content in Joining on smpaget blog

item.title: Joined Kintsugi
item.description:
When I left school, I entered into an apprenticeship within engineering construction. I was an apprentice Welder. I didn’t continue that career once I had competed my time and got some codings. I didn’t appreciate it at the time but welding was providing a long term meditation on joining. The act of taking one thing and joining it to another. The skill it takes to join tricky things. The preparation needed to join two things together. One of the parts of the process I often thought about was the Non Destructive Testing or NDT. The welds we did on certain artefacts would be loaded in a lorry and sent away for NDT. After a day or two the news come back to us that thing were ok. Only welds that didn’t pass the test was returned to us for destructive re working. Notably the weakest point of the welding process is the area just beside the weld. The join the weld makes is increasingly strong if done properly.

The practice of Kintsugi is a Japanese form of joining things, it uses gold where we used different metals, it brings strength and a symbolic meaning that displayed publicly. I was reminded of this joining practice by a scene in the latest Star Wars DVD the Rise of Skywalker. In the film a character repairs the helmet they destroyed during the last film in the series. The scene of repairing the helmet is very brief but the finished project reminds me of Kintsugi. And that is deliberate. The director of the film was going for t hat look. The idea of joining, welding, reflections upon Kintsugi seem to have something deep. Perhaps this peice sums up some of what I am thinking. The Kintsugi Generation - Makoto Fujimura

item.title: The Co-Operative - Good For Everyone
item.description: I have joined the Co-Operative. I like the idea of a business you join which then you can hold to account. The shares issues to me is very weird. Shares are a financial gamble. The idea that you buy a share and then you own a part of something which you can then trade with others to make money? apart from money their is no commitment towards the company you have the shares in. no partnership. Membership is a force for Good and change. It is also a commitment of intent in someway. A seeking to engage, be led and influenced while leading and influencing the organisation back. The Co-Operative have several campaigning strands to how they operate. I am not sure i agree with all of them, I am not familiar enough with them to say I support them all, but I do agree with the idea and principle of a business actually campaigning for something out with their own self interest. The benefits of membership are explained here You should join. Go on it’s literally free. Technorati Tags - The Co-Operative & Joining
